title: Christmas Leftovers Platter With Cranberry Mustard Sauce
summary: Want to know what to do with all your leftover Christmas vegetables? We've got just the recipe for you.

method.description: Preheat the oven to 200°C. Quarter the pickled onions.
Place the leftover veggies onto a tray with a drizzle of oil. Toss together, season with salt and pepper. Reheat in the oven, 10-15 mins until hot.
Meanwhile, pick the parsley leaves and discard the stalks. Season the crème fraîche generously with freshly cracked black pepper.
In a bowl, add the cranberry sauce, whole grain mustard, pickled onion brine and olive oil. Stir together, taste and season with salt, if needed.
Add the roasted veg into a bowl with the pickled onions and parsley leaves, toss together.
Spread the crème fraîche onto a platter or plate, top with the roasted veg, and drizzle over the cranberry mustard sauce.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
